title: pleopeltis angusta
intro: Pleopeltis angusta is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as a type of epiphytic or terrestrial fern native to Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Pleopeltis angusta are said to include wound healing, antioxidant activity, and potential antiviral properties, making it a subject of interest for further scientific research. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, although the specific preparation methods and dosages vary depending on the cultural and regional practices.
